variables.input:
fetch dt.entity.service
| fields managementZones
| expand managementZones
| dedup managementZones

0.title: Average ALL

1.title: Average of 75, 90, 95, 99

2.title: Average of Min,Max

3.title: Average of AVG/Median

4.title: Performance SLO

5.title: Availability SLO

6.title: SLO

8.title: Successful

10.title: Failed

11.title: Performance Insights by Service

12.title: Availability Insights by Service

13.title: Performance Insights by Endpoint

14.title: Availability Insights by Endpoint

15.title: Davis Auto-Baseline Availability Insights by Endpoint

16.title: Davis Auto-Baseline Performance Insights by Endpoint

17.title: Davis Auto-Baseline Availability Insights by Service

18.title: Davis Auto-Baseline Performance Insights by Service

20.title: Service DQL
20.query:
data record(services = """$Services""")
| fieldsAdd performance_dql = concat("timeseries total=avg(dt.service.request.response_time, default:0)
, by: { dt.entity.service }, filter: { in (dt.entity.service, {",services, "}) }
| fieldsAdd high=iCollectArray(if(total[]> (1000 * ",$SLI_Threshold,"), total[]))
| fieldsAdd low=iCollectArray(if(total[]<= (1000 * ",$SLI_Threshold,"), total[]))
| fieldsAdd highRespTimes=iCollectArray(if(isNull(high[]),0,else:1))
| fieldsAdd lowRespTimes=iCollectArray(if(isNull(low[]),0,else:1))
| fieldsAdd sli=100*(lowRespTimes[]/(lowRespTimes[]+highRespTimes[]))
| fieldsAdd entityName(dt.entity.service)
| fieldsRemove total, high, low, highRespTimes, lowRespTimes")
| fieldsadd availability_dql = concat("timeseries { total=sum(dt.service.request.count) ,failures=sum(dt.service.request.failure_count) }
  , by: { dt.entity.service }
  , filter: { in (dt.entity.service, {", services,"}) }
| fieldsAdd sli=(((total[]-failures[])/total[])*(100))
| fieldsAdd entityName(dt.entity.service)
| fieldsRemove total, failures")
| fields performance_dql, availability_dql

21.title: Endpoint DQL
21.query:
data record(endpoints = """$Endpoints""")
| fieldsAdd performance_dql = concat("timeseries total=avg(dt.service.request.response_time, default:0)
, by: { endpoint.name }, filter: { in (endpoint.name, {",endpoints, "}) }
| fieldsAdd high=iCollectArray(if(total[]> (1000 * ",$SLI_Threshold,"), total[]))
| fieldsAdd low=iCollectArray(if(total[]<= (1000 * ",$SLI_Threshold,"), total[]))
| fieldsAdd highRespTimes=iCollectArray(if(isNull(high[]),0,else:1))
| fieldsAdd lowRespTimes=iCollectArray(if(isNull(low[]),0,else:1))
| fieldsAdd sli=100*(lowRespTimes[]/(lowRespTimes[]+highRespTimes[]))
| fieldsRemove total, high, low, highRespTimes, lowRespTimes")
| fieldsadd availability_dql = concat("timeseries { total=sum(dt.service.request.count) ,failures=sum(dt.service.request.failure_count) }
  , by: { endpoint.name }
  , filter: { in (dt.entity.service, {", endpoints,"}) }
| fieldsAdd sli=(((total[]-failures[])/total[])*(100))
| fieldsRemove total, failures")
| fields performance_dql, availability_dql

22.content:
#### Service Indicator Intelligence

This dashboard delivers a focused analysis of Service Level Indicators (SLIs) across critical services, enabling teams to evaluate the reliability signals that underpin SLO performance. It empowers SREs, engineers, and stakeholders to explore how individual SLIs—such as latency, availability, and error rates—contribute to overall service health.

Use this dashboard to:

* Examine the performance of each SLI against defined thresholds.
* Identify which indicators are driving SLO breaches or successes.
* Detect early warning signs of service degradation.
* Support data-driven decisions for reliability improvements.

By surfacing granular insights into service behavior, this dashboard helps bridge the gap between operational metrics and strategic reliability goals.

---
